title: Fugazzeta Pizza Served on Traditional White Plates at a Historic Buenos Aires Pizzeria
description: Buenos Aires, Ciudad Autónoma de Buenos Aires: A close-up study of the iconic fugazzeta, an Argentine pizza variant characterized by its generous heap of sweet, translucent onions and melted mozzarella cheese. The shallow depth of field isolates the slice, emphasizing the bubbly, airy texture of the thick dough and the aromatic dusting of oregano. A secondary, out-of-focus slice rests on a matching plate in the background, anchoring the composition within the bustling, timeless setting of a traditional porteño eatery. Warm, ambient indoor lighting reflects off the ceramic surfaces, highlighting the glistening edges of the caramelized onion layers and the dough's golden-brown crust. This specific culinary staple represents the deep-rooted Italian influence on Argentine gastronomic identity, reflecting a cultural history shaped by waves of migration. Through the lens, the image captures the tactile simplicity of local comfort food, inviting the viewer into the sensory experience of a classic neighborhood pizzeria. Such culinary moments serve as essential benchmarks for travelers seeking to understand the soul of the city, as pizza here is more than a meal—it is a social institution. Every slice functions as a narrative of regional migration patterns and local adaptations that have defined the Argentine palate for over a century. By documenting these unpretentious dining encounters, we preserve the evolving legacy of South American urban culture and the enduring ritual of shared meals. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
